reason_for_decision: The article passes because it turns vague range language into a concrete procurement framework tied to task, target, geometry, and acceptance logic.
strengths: The article is particularly strong at separating detection, tracking, recognition, and identification so procurement language does not collapse different performance questions into one number.
It gives high decision value by translating abstract range claims into testable tender clauses and acceptance-test thinking.
The weak-versus-better clause examples make the guidance concrete without slipping into sales language.
summary_assessment: This is a strong applied procurement article. It is technically credible, highly usable, and gives buyers a much more defensible way to specify and test detection-range requirements.
